description: GraphQL introspection left enabled in production configuration exposes the full API schema to attackers.
fix_hint: Disable introspection in production: introspection: process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'.

description: Hardcoded __schema introspection query in non-test code may expose full API schema.
fix_hint: Remove __schema queries from production code; use schema-aware tooling only in development.

description: GraphQL server instantiated without query depth limiting middleware enables deeply nested DoS attacks.
fix_hint: Add graphql-depth-limit or depthLimit validation rule to the server configuration.

description: GraphQL server without query complexity analysis allows expensive queries to overwhelm the backend.
fix_hint: Add graphql-query-complexity or graphql-cost-analysis plugin to enforce complexity limits.

description: GraphQL batching enabled without per-batch limits allows brute-force and resource exhaustion attacks.
fix_hint: Set a maximum batch size or disable batching: allowBatchedHttpRequests: false.

description: GraphQL field suggestions in production reveal schema structure to attackers via typo correction.
fix_hint: Disable field suggestions in production by setting hideSchemaDetailsFromClientErrors: true.

description: GraphQL debug mode or playground enabled in production leaks schema and internal error details.
fix_hint: Gate playground/debug on NODE_ENV or FLASK_ENV, disabling in production.

description: GraphQL endpoint mounted without authentication middleware allows unauthenticated access to the API.
fix_hint: Add authentication middleware before the GraphQL handler: app.use('/graphql', authMiddleware, ...).

description: GraphQL allows unlimited aliases per query enabling brute-force attacks through alias-based batching.
fix_hint: Limit aliases per query using a custom validation rule or the graphql-armor maxAliases plugin.

description: GraphQL subscription resolver without rate limiting allows abuse via rapid subscription creation.
fix_hint: Apply rate limiting to subscription resolvers and cap the number of concurrent subscriptions per client.

description: GraphQL query constructed with string interpolation enables injection of arbitrary query fragments.
fix_hint: Use GraphQL variables instead of string interpolation to pass dynamic values into queries.

description: GraphQL query variables accepted without schema validation allow type confusion and injection.
fix_hint: Define strict GraphQL input types and let the schema enforce variable validation.

description: Persisted queries disabled allows arbitrary query strings increasing the attack surface for injection.
fix_hint: Enable automatic persisted queries (APQ) or use a query allowlist in production.

description: GraphQL error formatter exposes stack traces or internal error details to clients.
fix_hint: Strip stack traces and original errors from the formatError handler in production.

description: GraphQL CSRF prevention explicitly disabled allows cross-site mutation requests.
fix_hint: Enable csrfPrevention: true (default in Apollo Server 4+) and require a custom header.

description: GraphQL file upload handler without size or count limits enables resource exhaustion attacks.
fix_hint: Configure graphqlUploadExpress({ maxFileSize, maxFiles }) with appropriate limits.

description: Express GET route handler without auth middleware may expose data to unauthenticated users.
fix_hint: Add authentication middleware before the handler: router.get('/path', authMiddleware, handler).

description: Express POST route handler without auth middleware may allow unauthenticated state mutations.
fix_hint: Add authentication middleware: router.post('/path', authMiddleware, handler).

description: Express PUT route handler without auth middleware may allow unauthenticated resource updates.
fix_hint: Add authentication middleware: router.put('/path', authMiddleware, handler).

description: Express DELETE route handler without auth middleware may allow unauthenticated resource deletion.
fix_hint: Add authentication middleware: router.delete('/path', authMiddleware, handler).

description: Route handler accesses request and response but never checks user identity or permissions.
fix_hint: Add authorization check using req.user or a permission middleware before accessing resources.

description: Database record sent directly as response without field filtering exposes internal or sensitive data.
fix_hint: Select specific fields or use a serializer/DTO to control which properties are returned.

description: Database findAll without pagination limit can return unbounded result sets causing memory exhaustion.
fix_hint: Always apply a limit and offset: Model.findAll({ limit: maxPageSize, offset }).

description: MongoDB find without limit returns all matching documents, risking memory exhaustion on large collections.
fix_hint: Chain .limit() and .skip() to enforce pagination on all find queries.

description: Object.assign with request body overwrites model fields including protected ones like isAdmin or role.
fix_hint: Pick only allowed fields: const { name, email } = req.body; Object.assign(model, { name, email }).

description: Sending error stack traces in API responses reveals internal code structure to attackers.
fix_hint: Return a generic error message in production; log the full stack server-side only.

description: Exposing raw error messages in API responses may reveal internal implementation details.
fix_hint: Return user-friendly error messages; map internal errors to safe response codes.

description: Stack trace exposed in response without NODE_ENV production check leaks implementation details.
fix_hint: Only include stack traces when NODE_ENV !== 'production'.

description: API route mounted without version prefix (/api/v1) makes breaking changes harder to manage safely.
fix_hint: Prefix routes with a version: app.use('/api/v1', router).

description: Bulk database operation directly from request body without size limit enables resource exhaustion.
fix_hint: Validate req.body.length before bulk operations and enforce a maximum batch size.

description: Request handler without content-type validation may process unexpected payload formats.
fix_hint: Validate Content-Type header before processing the request body.

description: Login endpoint without rate limiting allows credential stuffing and brute-force attacks.
fix_hint: Apply rate limiting to login: app.post('/login', rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 5 }), handler).

description: Registration endpoint without rate limiting allows mass account creation.
fix_hint: Apply rate limiting to registration with a strict per-IP window.

description: Password reset endpoint without rate limiting allows email bombing and token enumeration.
fix_hint: Apply strict rate limiting to password reset endpoints.

description: Express application without any rate-limiting package is vulnerable to denial-of-service.
fix_hint: Install and configure express-rate-limit as global middleware.

description: Rate limiter configured without standard rate limit response headers prevents clients from adapting.
fix_hint: Enable standardHeaders: true in rate limiter to send RateLimit-* headers.

description: Rate limiter keyed on X-Forwarded-For header can be bypassed by spoofing the header.
fix_hint: Use a trusted proxy configuration (app.set('trust proxy', 1)) or rate-limit on authenticated user ID.

description: File upload endpoint without rate limiting allows resource exhaustion via repeated large uploads.
fix_hint: Apply rate limiting before the file upload middleware.

description: OTP/2FA verification without rate limiting allows brute-force of short codes in seconds.
fix_hint: Rate limit OTP verification to 3-5 attempts per window with exponential backoff.

description: Email sending function without rate limiting enables email bombing and spam abuse.
fix_hint: Rate limit email-triggering endpoints and implement per-recipient cooldowns.

description: Flask application without flask-limiter is vulnerable to denial-of-service on all endpoints.
fix_hint: Install flask-limiter and apply default rate limits: Limiter(app, default_limits=['200/day']).

description: Express CORS with wildcard origin and credentials leaks cookies and auth tokens to any domain.
fix_hint: Replace wildcard origin with an explicit allowlist when credentials: true is set.

description: Express CORS reflecting the request Origin header effectively allows any domain.
fix_hint: Validate the Origin header against an explicit allowlist before reflecting it.

description: Flask-CORS with wildcard origins on all resources allows any domain to access the API.
fix_hint: Replace the wildcard with a list of allowed origins: origins=['https://myapp.com'].

description: Django CORS allows all origins with credentials, exposing session cookies to any domain.
fix_hint: Set CORS_ALLOW_ALL_ORIGINS = False and use CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS with an explicit list.

description: Spring @CrossOrigin with wildcard origin allows any domain to call the annotated endpoint.
fix_hint: Replace * with specific origins: @CrossOrigin(origins = "https://myapp.com").

description: Rails CORS with wildcard origin and credentials enabled leaks auth tokens to any domain.
fix_hint: Replace wildcard origin with an explicit allowlist when credentials are enabled.

description: CORS allowing 'null' origin can be exploited via sandboxed iframes and data: URIs.
fix_hint: Remove 'null' from the allowed origins list as it can be spoofed.

description: Dynamic CORS origin handler without an explicit allowlist check may permit unintended origins.
fix_hint: Validate origins against an explicit allowlist array in the dynamic origin callback.

description: WebSocket server without verifyClient callback allows connections from any origin (CSWSH).
fix_hint: Add a verifyClient callback to validate the Origin header against an allowlist.

description: WebSocket connection handler without authentication allows anonymous persistent connections.
fix_hint: Authenticate the WebSocket upgrade request via token or session before accepting the connection.

description: WebSocket broadcasting raw message data to all clients can spread XSS or injected content.
fix_hint: Sanitize and validate message content before broadcasting to other clients.

description: WebSocket connection over unencrypted ws:// to non-local host exposes data to interception.
fix_hint: Use wss:// for all non-local WebSocket connections to encrypt traffic.

description: WebSocket server without maxPayload limit allows oversized messages to exhaust memory.
fix_hint: Set maxPayload in WebSocket server options: new WebSocketServer({ maxPayload: 1024 * 1024 }).

description: WebSocket message handler without rate limiting allows message flooding denial-of-service.
fix_hint: Implement per-connection message rate limiting using a token bucket or sliding window.

description: WebSocket auto-reconnection without re-authentication may resume expired or revoked sessions.
fix_hint: Re-authenticate the WebSocket connection on every reconnection attempt.

description: WebSocket upgrade relying only on cookies without CSRF token is vulnerable to cross-site hijacking.
fix_hint: Require a CSRF token or separate auth token in the WebSocket handshake query string.

description: gRPC server reflection enabled in production reveals all service definitions to attackers.
fix_hint: Disable gRPC reflection in production builds; enable only in development.

description: gRPC channel or server without TLS transmits data including auth tokens in plaintext.
fix_hint: Use grpc.ssl_channel_credentials() or ServerCredentials.createSsl() for TLS.

description: gRPC server created without authentication interceptor allows unauthenticated RPC calls.
fix_hint: Add an authentication interceptor: grpc.server(pool, interceptors=[AuthInterceptor()]).

description: gRPC server without message size limit allows oversized payloads to exhaust memory.
fix_hint: Set max_receive_message_length to a reasonable limit (e.g. 4MB).

description: Credentials sent over a plaintext gRPC channel are transmitted unencrypted on the wire.
fix_hint: Never send credentials over insecure channels; use TLS for all authenticated gRPC connections.

description: gRPC call without deadline or timeout can hang indefinitely consuming server resources.
fix_hint: Always set a deadline on gRPC calls: stub.Method(request, timeout=5) or context.WithTimeout.

description: gRPC server without keepalive settings may accumulate idle connections exhausting file descriptors.
fix_hint: Configure keepalive settings to detect and close idle connections.

description: Using context.Background without timeout in a gRPC handler can lead to indefinite blocking.
fix_hint: Use context.WithTimeout or context.WithDeadline to limit request processing time.

description: JSON body parser without size limit allows oversized payloads to crash the server with OOM.
fix_hint: Set a body size limit: express.json({ limit: '100kb' }).

description: File upload middleware without size limits allows arbitrarily large uploads to exhaust disk or memory.
fix_hint: Set limits: multer({ limits: { fileSize: 5 * 1024 * 1024 } }).

description: Route handler does not check Content-Type allowing unexpected payload formats.
fix_hint: Validate Content-Type header or use middleware that rejects unexpected types.

description: Iterating over request body array without length check allows payload-based DoS.
fix_hint: Validate array length before iteration: if (req.body.length > MAX_ITEMS) return res.status(400).

description: String fields from request body accepted without length validation may cause storage or processing issues.
fix_hint: Validate string length using a schema validator like Joi, Zod, or express-validator.

description: HTTP server without request timeout allows slow-read (Slowloris) denial-of-service attacks.
fix_hint: Set server.timeout and server.headersTimeout to prevent slow request attacks.

description: XML parser instantiated without explicitly disabling external entities is vulnerable to XXE.
fix_hint: Disable external entity processing in the parser configuration.

description: File upload without extension or MIME type whitelist allows upload of executable files.
fix_hint: Add a fileFilter to multer that validates extension and MIME type against an allowlist.

description: Financial transaction endpoint without idempotency key may process duplicate requests.
fix_hint: Require an Idempotency-Key header and deduplicate requests server-side.

description: Sensitive data passed as URL parameter is logged by proxies, browsers, and access logs.
fix_hint: Pass sensitive data in request headers or POST body instead of URL parameters.

description: Express app without HSTS header allows downgrade attacks from HTTPS to HTTP.
fix_hint: Add HSTS header via helmet or manually: res.setHeader('Strict-Transport-Security', 'max-age=31536000').

description: State-changing endpoint without CSRF protection may be exploited by cross-site request forgery.
fix_hint: Add CSRF protection using csurf middleware or SameSite cookie attributes.

description: Query parameters used without validation allow injection of unexpected keys and values.
fix_hint: Validate query parameters with a schema library like Joi, Zod, or express-validator.

description: Route using user-supplied resource ID without ownership check is vulnerable to IDOR.
fix_hint: Verify the authenticated user owns or has permission to access the requested resource.

description: gRPC client using insecure credentials communicates without TLS encryption.
fix_hint: Use grpc.credentials.createSsl() for encrypted communication.

description: Go gRPC client dialing with WithInsecure sends all traffic including credentials in cleartext.
fix_hint: Use grpc.WithTransportCredentials(credentials.NewTLS(tlsConfig)) for TLS.
